text: The best among you are those who have the best manners and character.
None of you truly believes until he wishes for his brother what he wishes for himself.
The strong man is not the one who can overpower others. The strong man is the one who controls himself when he is angry.
Make things easy and do not make them difficult. Give good tidings and do not drive people away.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him speak good or remain silent.
The best of people are those who are most beneficial to people.
A Muslim is the one from whose tongue and hands other Muslims are safe.
Allah does not look at your bodies or your forms, but He looks at your hearts and your deeds.
Smiling in the face of your brother is an act of charity.
Be in this world as if you were a stranger or a traveler.
Richness is not having many possessions. Rather, true richness is the richness of the soul.
The believer does not taunt others, nor curse others, nor is he immoral or shameless.
Kindness is a mark of faith, and whoever is not kind has no faith.
Every act of kindness is charity.
Do not belittle any good deed, even meeting your brother with a cheerful face.
The most beloved of deeds to Allah are those that are most consistent, even if they are small.
Whoever is not grateful to people, is not grateful to Allah.
He who eats his fill while his neighbor goes without food is not a believer.
When you see a person who has been given more than you in money and beauty, look to those who have been given less.

Take advantage of five before five: your youth before your old age, your health before your sickness, your wealth before your poverty, your free time before your busyness, and your life before your death.
The most perfect of the believers in faith is the one who is best in character.
Modesty is part of faith.
Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.
Leave that which makes you doubt for that which does not make you doubt.
Feed the hungry, visit the sick, and set free the captives.
A good word is charity.
The ink of the scholar is holier than the blood of the martyr.
He is not one of us who does not have mercy on our young and does not respect our elders.
The world is a prison for the believer and a paradise for the disbeliever.
Verily, Allah is beautiful and He loves beauty.
The best charity is that given in Ramadan.
Whoever builds a mosque for Allah, Allah will build for him a house in Paradise.

The two feet of a servant will not move on the Day of Judgment until he is asked about his life and how he spent it, his knowledge and what he did with it, his wealth and how he earned and spent it, and his body and how he used it.
Allah will shade seven types of people under His shade on the Day when there will be no shade but His.
The supplication is the essence of worship.
Paradise lies at the feet of your mother.
Cleanliness is half of faith.
Whoever treads a path in search of knowledge, Allah will make easy for him the path to Paradise.
The one who looks after a widow or a poor person is like a warrior who fights for Allah's cause.
Beware of envy, for envy consumes good deeds just as fire consumes wood.
No fatigue, nor disease, nor sorrow, nor sadness, nor hurt, nor distress befalls a Muslim, even if it were the prick he receives from a thorn, but that Allah expiates some of his sins for that.
Whoever removes a worldly hardship from a believer, Allah will remove from him one of the hardships of the Day of Resurrection.
The best of you are those who learn the Quran and teach it.
Part of the perfection of a person's Islam is his leaving that which does not concern him.
Speak the truth even if it is bitter.
Allah is gentle and loves gentleness in all things.
The best form of worship is awaiting relief with patience.
Whoever has an atom's weight of arrogance in his heart will not enter Paradise.
The believer is not stung from the same hole twice.
Give the laborer his wages before his sweat dries.
The merciful are shown mercy by the Most Merciful. Show mercy to those on earth, and the One above the heavens will show mercy to you.
When a man dies, his deeds come to an end except for three things: ongoing charity, knowledge which is beneficial, or a righteous child who prays for him.
Tie your camel and then put your trust in Allah.
The best of houses is a house where an orphan is treated well.
He who is deprived of gentleness is deprived of all good.
Do not waste water even if you are at a running stream.
The believers, in their mutual kindness, compassion, and sympathy, are just like one body. When one limb suffers, the whole body responds with sleeplessness and fever.
Actions are judged by intentions, and every person will be rewarded according to their intention.
Whoever is patient, Allah will give him patience, and no one is given a gift better and more comprehensive than patience.
There is a reward for kindness to every living thing.
